VF-OVERVIEW.title: VF-OVERVIEW — Overview & Conventions
VF-OVERVIEW.conjecture: This document defines the shared vocabulary, coordinate conventions, and teaching methodology for the entire VR Endless Forest project. It is the conceptual foundation — if the overview is wrong, every other spec inherits its confusion.

VF-ARCH.title: VF-ARCH — Architecture
VF-ARCH.conjecture: The architecture spec claims that a client-side WebXR application can be cleanly decomposed into independent systems (terrain, water, forest, atmosphere, weather, audio, movement) with well-defined data flow and a deterministic frame update order. The dolly camera rig and A-Frame entity structure are sufficient for both desktop and VR.

VF-TERRAIN.title: VF-TERRAIN — Terrain System
VF-TERRAIN.conjecture: An infinite, seamless terrain can be generated from 13 deterministic noise instances with chunked LOD, producing believable mountains, valleys, and stream channels — all at 90 fps on Quest 3. The 4-part height calculation (base fBm + stream carving + mountains + river carving) produces no visible seams at chunk boundaries.

VF-WATER.title: VF-WATER — Water System
VF-WATER.conjecture: A single 300×300m water plane with 13 sine waves, GPU-side displacement, and marching-squares shore foam can convincingly simulate an ocean surface, shoreline, and swimming mechanics — without any physics engine or fluid simulation.

VF-FOREST.title: VF-FOREST — Forest System
VF-FOREST.conjecture: Four procedural tree types (pine, oak, birch, tussock) placed on a noise-driven density grid, with altitude-zone scaling, stream exclusion, and wind animation, can create the illusion of an endless forest — using only instanced geometry and vertex colours, no textures.

VF-WILDLIFE.title: VF-WILDLIFE — Wildlife
VF-WILDLIFE.conjecture: Bird flocks following simple orbital paths and peek encounters (eyes behind trees) are sufficient to make the forest feel alive, without requiring AI pathfinding, physics, or complex animation systems.

VF-ATMOSPHERE.title: VF-ATMOSPHERE — Atmosphere
VF-ATMOSPHERE.conjecture: A procedural sky dome with real astronomical calculations (solar declination, hour angle, altitude), 2865 deterministic stars, planetary bodies, clouds, fog, fireflies, and shooting stars can produce a convincing day/night cycle that matches the user's real-world time and location.

VF-WEATHER.title: VF-WEATHER — Weather System
VF-WEATHER.conjecture: A simple 3-state machine (sunny/cloudy/rainy) driving a single weatherIntensity float (0–2) is sufficient to produce convincing weather transitions, including rain particles, lightning, thunder, ground wetness, and coordinated changes across all visual and audio systems.

VF-AUDIO.title: VF-AUDIO — Audio System
VF-AUDIO.conjecture: All forest soundscape can be generated procedurally using Web Audio API oscillators, filters, and noise buffers — no audio files needed. Footsteps, crickets, birds, rain, thunder, wind, and water sounds emerge from synthesis parameters alone.

VF-MOVEMENT.title: VF-MOVEMENT — Movement & Input
VF-MOVEMENT.conjecture: A unified movement system can handle desktop WASD, VR thumbstick, terrain following, tree collision, swimming, jumping, and ski physics — all sharing the same ground-truth height function and collision model, with no physics engine.

VF-COLLECTIBLES.title: VF-COLLECTIBLES — Collectibles & HUD
VF-COLLECTIBLES.conjecture: Deterministically-placed glowing orbs on a noise-driven grid, with collection scoring, sprint power, persistence via localStorage, and a HUD with minimap, provide sufficient gameplay to motivate exploration of the endless forest.

VF-PERFORMANCE.title: VF-PERFORMANCE — Performance Budget
VF-PERFORMANCE.conjecture: The entire scene — terrain, water, 3000+ trees, weather particles, audio, wildlife — can run at 90 fps on Quest 3 (72 fps minimum) within a draw call budget of <150 and triangle budget of <500K, using instancing, LOD, and aggressive culling.

VF-CONFIG.title: VF-CONFIG — Configuration Reference
VF-CONFIG.conjecture: Every tunable parameter in the entire project can be captured in a single flat table of ~250 key-value pairs. This table is the single source of truth — if a parameter exists in code but not in VF-CONFIG, it is a bug.

VF-VERIFY.title: VF-VERIFY — Verification Guide
VF-VERIFY.conjecture: A set of visual spot-checks, gotcha callouts, and per-system checklists is sufficient to verify that an implementation matches the specifications — without requiring automated test infrastructure.
